old_claim.note: Open Source Software supports manufacturing, deployment, commercialization, or operational scaling.

new_claim.note: Open-source dependencies and package ecosystems are a major software-supply-chain security scope, especially for dependency governance, provenance, and third-party component controls.

source_shape.source_locator: NIST EO 14028 software supply-chain guidance includes open-source software controls and supply-chain risk practices for software producers and federal acquisition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source treats open-source software controls as part of software supply-chain security guidance.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge remains a scaling dependency because open-source ecosystems broaden the dependency and package-governance surface that supply-chain controls manage.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: open_source_software remains a commercial_or_scaling_dependency with stronger source support.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to proprietary-only software delivery.
A stronger source showed open-source components are out of scope for software supply-chain security.
The graph gains a more precise package_registries or dependency_management node that should replace this broad prerequisite.
short_reason: Open-source dependencies are a central software supply-chain security scope.
